description: A posted notice, hand gesture, or readable mark can be a 'kiroa' (sign) when it is used to convey information. 'siru' (marker) can identify a place simply by being there, while 'relo' (symbol) stands for a further meaning through convention. Indicating or signifying uses a verb; the sign itself remains a noun.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-front 'i'. Tap or briefly trill at the alveolar ridge before stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Hold the syllable break and finish on open central 'a'.
usage_notes: The compound registry uses 'thekiro kiroa' for a written character or letter. A sign can be read with 'theo' when it carries readable wording or an understood visual system.

semantic_domains.communication: a perceivable form used to convey information or instruction
semantic_domains.creation: a communicative object or mark deliberately made or placed
